title: Cyperus melanacme

taxonomy: Cyperus melanacme is classified within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family is part of the order Poales, which includes various monocotyledonous flowering plants. Cyperus melanacme's classification highlights its position among other monocotyledonous plants.

intro: Cyperus melanacme, also known as Black Sedge, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae, characterized by its dark, almost black stems and small, yellowish-brown flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to treat digestive issues and reduce fever. Bioactive constituents of Cyperus melanacme include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cyperus melanacme is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
